task_description: Screen each abstract for relevance to: machine learning for early detection or diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's, ALS, Huntington's, or related conditions). Label RELEVANT if the paper applies ML to detect, classify, or diagnose a neurodegenerative condition from any data modality. Label NOT_RELEVANT if the ML task is prognosis, treatment selection, mechanism discovery, or unrelated disease.

papers.title: Multi-Modal Graph Neural Network for Alzheimer's Disease Diagnosis from MRI and PET
papers.abstract: We construct heterogeneous graphs over brain regions using both structural MRI volumes and FDG-PET SUVRs from 720 ADNI participants. A multi-modal graph neural network aggregates features across modalities to classify cognitively normal, MCI, and Alzheimer's disease subjects. Three-way accuracy 0.87 is achieved, outperforming unimodal baselines and random forest. Attention weights confirm amyloid-affected regions dominate classification.
papers.topic_hint: GNN, Alzheimer's disease, MRI, PET, ADNI, multi-modal diagnosis
papers.methodology_hint: multi-modal GNN, ADNI, accuracy 0.87, three-way AD classification, attention weights
papers.claimed_contribution: Multi-modal GNN for AD diagnosis from MRI+PET, accuracy 0.87 with amyloid-region attention.

papers.title: Causal Inference for Identifying Neuroprotective Factors Against Parkinson's Disease
papers.abstract: Observational data offer opportunities to identify neuroprotective lifestyle factors if confounding is controlled. We apply doubly-robust causal inference to 65,000 participants from UK Biobank to estimate protective effects of physical activity, coffee consumption, and NSAID use on Parkinson's disease incidence. Causal effect estimates differ substantially from unadjusted associations. No ML classifier or diagnostic model is built.
papers.topic_hint: causal inference, Parkinson's disease, neuroprotection, UK Biobank, observational
papers.methodology_hint: doubly-robust causal inference, UK Biobank, PD incidence — causal analysis not ML diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: Causal estimates of neuroprotective factors for PD from UK Biobank, no diagnostic ML model.

papers.title: Gait Asymmetry Features and SVM for Early Parkinson's Detection from Wearable Sensors
papers.abstract: Motor asymmetry precedes clinical Parkinson's diagnosis by years. We extract 42 gait asymmetry features from three-axis accelerometers worn by 180 early-stage Parkinson's and 180 age-matched healthy participants over 7-day free-living monitoring periods. An SVM trained on these features achieves accuracy 0.83 and AUC 0.88 distinguishing early PD from controls, with sensitivity 0.82 at 90% specificity.
papers.topic_hint: SVM, gait analysis, Parkinson's detection, wearable, accelerometer
papers.methodology_hint: SVM, gait asymmetry features, 7-day wearable, accuracy 0.83, AUC 0.88, early PD detection
papers.claimed_contribution: SVM for early Parkinson's detection from wearable gait features, AUC 0.88, sensitivity 0.82.

papers.title: Deep Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Clinical Trial Design in Alzheimer's Research
papers.abstract: Adaptive trial designs can reduce sample sizes for Alzheimer's drug trials by enriching enrolment of likely progressors. We apply deep reinforcement learning to optimise adaptive randomisation and stopping rules in simulated amyloid-positive MCI trials. The RL policy reduces expected sample size by 28% while preserving statistical power. This paper concerns trial design, not diagnostic or detection algorithms.
papers.topic_hint: reinforcement learning, adaptive clinical trial, Alzheimer's, trial design
papers.methodology_hint: deep RL, adaptive trial design, Alzheimer's simulation — trial design not diagnosis
papers.claimed_contribution: RL for adaptive AD trial design, 28% sample reduction, no diagnostic ML.

papers.title: LSTM on Longitudinal Neuropsychological Test Scores for Predicting MCI-to-AD Conversion
papers.abstract: Longitudinal cognitive test trajectories carry diagnostic information beyond single assessments. We train an LSTM on sequences of MMSE, MoCA, and ADAS-Cog scores from 3,200 ADNI participants tracked over 4 years to predict conversion from mild cognitive impairment to Alzheimer's disease within 24 months. AUC 0.83 is achieved, outperforming the last-observation-only baseline by 9 points.
papers.topic_hint: LSTM, MCI-to-AD conversion, neuropsychological testing, ADNI, longitudinal
papers.methodology_hint: LSTM, MMSE/MoCA/ADAS-Cog sequences, ADNI, AUC 0.83, longitudinal cognitive testing
papers.claimed_contribution: LSTM for MCI-to-AD conversion prediction from longitudinal neuropsychological scores, AUC 0.83.

papers.title: Recurrent Neural Networks for Mortality Prediction in Mechanical Ventilation Patients
papers.abstract: We train RNNs on minute-resolution ventilator waveforms and ABG data from 22,000 MIMIC-IV mechanically ventilated episodes to predict 30-day mortality. AUROC 0.84 is achieved, significantly outperforming static SOFA score. The work focuses on ICU critical care mortality, not neurodegenerative disease detection.
papers.topic_hint: RNN, mechanical ventilation, ICU mortality, MIMIC-IV, SOFA
papers.methodology_hint: RNN, ventilator waveforms, MIMIC-IV, AUROC 0.84, ICU mortality — not neurodegeneration
papers.claimed_contribution: RNN for ventilated patient mortality prediction, AUROC 0.84, outperforming SOFA.

papers.title: Convolutional Neural Network for ALS Diagnosis from Eye-Tracking and Speech Biomarkers
papers.abstract: Amyotrophic lateral sclerosis affects both motor and bulbar function. We extract eye movement irregularity scores and acoustic speech features from 160 ALS patients and 160 healthy controls recorded during tablet-based assessment sessions. A CNN trained on the combined feature map achieves accuracy 0.81 and AUC 0.87. Saccadic intrusions in eye-tracking and reduced pitch range in speech emerge as early biomarkers.
papers.topic_hint: CNN, ALS diagnosis, eye-tracking, speech biomarkers, acoustic features
papers.methodology_hint: CNN, eye movement + speech features, 160 ALS + 160 controls, accuracy 0.81, AUC 0.87
papers.claimed_contribution: CNN for ALS diagnosis from eye-tracking and speech, AUC 0.87, with biomarker identification.

papers.title: Federated Machine Learning for Genomic Variant Pathogenicity Classification
papers.abstract: Rare variant pathogenicity classification requires diverse datasets held across institutions. We implement federated learning to train an ensemble model on variant consequence annotations from ClinVar, gnomAD, and 8 hospital genomic programs. The federated model matches centralised performance on ClinGen benchmarks for BRCA1/2 and Lynch syndrome variants. This work is about germline variant classification, not neurodegenerative disease diagnosis.
papers.topic_hint: federated learning, variant pathogenicity, ClinVar, genomics, germline
papers.methodology_hint: federated learning, ClinVar/gnomAD, BRCA1/2 — genomic variant not neurodegeneration
papers.claimed_contribution: Federated variant pathogenicity classifier matching centralised performance on ClinGen benchmarks.

papers.title: Speech Analysis with Transformer Encoder for Early Alzheimer's Detection
papers.abstract: Subtle language deficits appear in spontaneous speech years before clinical Alzheimer's diagnosis. We fine-tune a Wav2Vec 2.0 encoder on 1,800 speech samples from the DementiaBank Pitt Corpus to classify Alzheimer's disease versus healthy ageing. The model achieves F1 0.79, outperforming hand-crafted acoustic and linguistic feature baselines. Explainability analysis identifies shorter utterance lengths, more fillers, and reduced lexical diversity as discriminative signals.
papers.topic_hint: speech analysis, Wav2Vec, Alzheimer's detection, DementiaBank, Pitt Corpus
papers.methodology_hint: Wav2Vec 2.0, DementiaBank Pitt, F1 0.79, acoustic/linguistic baselines, early AD detection
papers.claimed_contribution: Wav2Vec for early AD detection from speech, F1 0.79, with linguistic signal explainability.

papers.title: Spatiotemporal Graph Neural Networks for Crowd Flow Prediction in Urban Environments
papers.abstract: Urban crowd density forecasting supports emergency response and event planning. We develop a spatiotemporal GNN trained on metro station passenger counts and GPS mobility traces from four cities to predict 30-minute-ahead crowd flow. Mean absolute error of 18 persons per cell is achieved. No biomedical, clinical, or neurodegenerative data is involved.
papers.topic_hint: spatiotemporal GNN, crowd flow prediction, urban mobility, metro
papers.methodology_hint: spatiotemporal GNN, metro crowd flow, urban mobility — no biomedical or neurodegeneration
papers.claimed_contribution: Spatiotemporal GNN for urban crowd flow prediction, MAE 18 persons, no biomedical connection.
